Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar May-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Gehring, Esq. 310-264-7744 of Thomas Gehring & Associates for Plaintiff.
Anthony Giles, Esq. 415-839-2099 for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares it's views and indicates other requests have been withdrawn and only the issue as to Ms. Cooke remains. Defendant argues. Plaintiff responds.
(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOHN MILLWARD's Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling and is modified as to attorney's fees.Plaintiff John P. Millward's application for a right to attach order and order for issuance of a writ of attachment against Defendant Treva Cooke is granted in the amount of $115,361.45.

Code of Civil Procedure section 484.010 provides for issuance of a right to attach order and writ of attachment upon filing of the complaint or any time thereafter. A court "shall issue a right to attach order" if it finds: (1) the claim upon which the attachment is based is one upon which an attachment may be issued; (2) the plaintiff has established the probable validity of the claim upon which the attachment is based; (3) the attachment is not sought for a purpose other than the recovery on the claim upon which the attachment is based; and (4) the amount to be secured by the attachment is greater than zero. (Code Civ. Proc. 484.090(a).) Plaintiff has satisfied these criteria. Code of Civil Procedure section 483.010 allows for prejudgment attachment in actions involving claims for money damages not less than $500 that are based on contracts, so the first element is met. As explained below, Plaintiff has established probable validity of his claim under the Guaranty. Ms. Cooke has not identified any improper purpose for Plaintiff seeking attachment. And Plaintiff is seeking $115,361.45 plus attorneys' fees, an amount greater than zero.

Ms. Cooke has not identified any reason why attachment is inappropriate. Her requests for additional discovery prior to attachment are inapposite as she has not identified a statutory requirement making discovery a prerequisite to attachment. Nor is there any need for Plaintiff to demonstrate urgency. Ms. Cooke's argument about her liability being conditioned on a finding of liability as to Volition also fails. Under the Guaranty, Ms. Cooke agreed to "unconditionally guarantee to [Plaintiff], irrespective of the genuineness, validity, regularity or enforceability of the Note or any other circumstances, the prompt payment of the initial $250,000 in principal paid under the Note, by acceleration or otherwise." (Millward Decl. 11, Exh. C.) "A guaranty is a separate and independent obligation from that of the principal debt." (United Central Bank v. Superior Court (2009) 179 Cal.App.4th 212, 215.) The unequivocal language of the Guaranty and the legal authority establishing that Ms. Cooke has an independent obligation to secure the initial $250,000.00 regardless of liability under the other contracts show the probable validity of Plaintiff's claim supporting his right to attachment. Therefore, Plaintiff is entitled to the requested attachment of $115,361.45, the outstanding amount of the $250,000.00 principal guaranteed by Ms. Cooke.
(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
